description:
You are helping a developer tidy up a small Python project that lives in:

/home/user/project

The relevant structure that already exists when you start is:

/home/user/project/src/main.py  
/home/user/project/src/utils.py  
/home/user/project/src/db.py  
/home/user/project/logs/            (an empty directory that already exists)

Each of those three *.py* files begins with exactly two comment lines in the following style:

Author: <Full Name>  
Last Modified: <YYYY-MM-DD>

Goal
----
Perform three text-processing steps – all of which must rely on **awk and/or sed** (no other languages, no Python code) – so that the project ends in the state described below.

1. Build a CSV called  
   /home/user/project/authors.csv  
   The file must contain one line per *.py* source file, sorted **alphabetically by the filename** (i.e. *db.py*, *main.py*, *utils.py*).  
   The comma-separated columns must be in this exact order with no extra whitespace:  

       filename,author,last_modified

   Therefore the final authors.csv **must look exactly like this** (newline after every line, including the last one):

       db.py,Alice Brown,2023-05-10
       main.py,John Doe,2023-05-12
       utils.py,Jane Smith,2023-05-11

2. Normalise indentation inside every *.py* file under /home/user/project/src by replacing each tab character (ASCII 0x09) with four spaces.  Do this **in-place**.

3. Append a single summary line to  
   /home/user/project/logs/processing.log  
   If the file does not yet exist, create it.  
   The line must be appended (so older content, if any, is preserved) and must read **exactly**:

       Metadata extracted for 3 files

   followed by a newline at the end.

Constraints
-----------
• Use only standard shell utilities, limiting yourself to 3-4 shell commands total; awk and sed must do the heavy lifting.  
• Do **not** hardcode absolute paths to Python files inside your commands – discover them programmatically, so the solution still works if new files are added later.  
• Do not modify or delete any other files or directories.  
• The automated grader will check:  
  1. authors.csv exists with the exact content and order shown above.  
  2. /home/user/project/src/*.py files no longer contain any tab characters.  
  3. /home/user/project/logs/processing.log ends with the required summary line.

No additional output is required beyond creating/modifying the files described.
truth:
Initial project structure (present BEFORE the agent starts)
-----------------------------------------------------------
/home/user/project/src/main.py
--------------------------------
"""  
Author: John Doe  
Last Modified: 2023-05-12  
"""  

def main():	    # contains one literal tab before “main”
	print("Hello World")	# line begins with one literal tab

/home/user/project/src/utils.py
--------------------------------
"""  
Author: Jane Smith  
Last Modified: 2023-05-11  
"""  

def helper():	    # one tab before “helper”
	print("Utility")	# one tab

/home/user/project/src/db.py
-----------------------------
"""  
Author: Alice Brown  
Last Modified: 2023-05-10  
"""  

def connect():	    # one tab
	print("DB connect")	# one tab

/home/user/project/logs/   (empty directory)

Expected state AFTER the agent finishes
---------------------------------------
1. /home/user/project/authors.csv exists and its full content is:

db.py,Alice Brown,2023-05-10
main.py,John Doe,2023-05-12
utils.py,Jane Smith,2023-05-11

(three newline-terminated lines, exactly as shown)

2. No tab characters (ASCII 0x09) remain anywhere inside  
   /home/user/project/src/main.py, utils.py or db.py.

3. /home/user/project/logs/processing.log exists (or has grown) and its **last** line is exactly:

Metadata extracted for 3 files

(with a trailing newline).

The grader will:
• `grep -P "\t" -r /home/user/project/src` and expect zero output.  
• `cat /home/user/project/authors.csv` and compare against the three-line literal above.  
• `tail -n 1 /home/user/project/logs/processing.log` and expect the exact summary line.
